ddls:
CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the forecast_targets table */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the forecast (e.g., US, state, region) */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast type */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of forecast (e.g., category, numerical) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the forecast */,
  "confidence_interval" REAL /* Confidence interval for numerical forecasts */,
  "probability" REAL /* Probability of different forecast outcomes */,
  "is_public" INTEGER /* Whether the forecast is publicly available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES forecast_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_type FOREIGN KEY ("type") REFERENCES categories ("category_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_target_id FOREIGN KEY ("target_id") REFERENCES forecasts_targets ("target_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts_targets" (
  "target_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast target */,
  "target_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecast target */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the forecast target */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("target_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region of the location (e.g., North, South) */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the location */,
  "parent_location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table for hierarchical structure */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_parent_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the forecast type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the forecast type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color associated with the category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the roles table */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the forecasts table */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date of the notification */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Message of the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_notifications_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_hierarchy" (
  "hierarchy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each hierarchy entry */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table */,
  "parent_location_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the locations table for hierarchical structure */,
  "hierarchy_level" INTEGER /* Level of the location in the hierarchy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("hierarchy_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* ID of the forecast that was changed */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user who made the change */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., update, delete) */,
  "old_value" REAL /* Old value of the forecast */,
  "new_value" REAL /* New value of the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_history_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
